name: Cochinita Pibil
recipeIngredient: 4 Pounds pork butt, cut into 2-inch Chunks
1 Pound banana leaves, softened over low Flame, or Foil
4 Anaheim chiles, roasted, peeled and Sliced into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, mash together the achiote paste, garlic, orange juice, lime juice, bay leaves, cumin, cinnamon, thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper using a fork.
2. Add the pork to the mixture and toss until evenly coated. Marinate at room temperature for at least 4 hours.
3. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Heat a dry cast-iron skillet over high heat. Char the onions until blackened on both sides, then char the tomatoes on both sides. Set aside.
5. Line a large baking dish with one layer of banana leaves or foil. Arrange the pork in an even layer and top with the onions, tomatoes, chiles, and all the marinade. Cover with more banana leaves and wrap the dish tightly in foil.
6. Bake for 2 to 2.5 hours, or until the pork is tender and moist. Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes.
7. Unwrap and serve with pickled shallots.
Yield: 8 to 10 servings
